behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the LLM actually grounding the draft in thread details; a mocked model or canned output can pass while real generations drift generic.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real model call with a thread containing distinct, checkable points (e.g., dates, asks, constraints) and assert the draft explicitly addresses multiple thread-specific points while a generic baseline would fail.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If the model is mocked, this failure mode is hidden; the test must exercise real model judgment.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This is mainly about wiring claim extraction + KB lookup + contradiction logic; in-process unit tests can miss failures at module boundaries (schema mismatches, retrieval misses).
behaviors.test_description: Use real local KB/storage and checker pipeline with a draft containing known contradictory claims; verify contradictions are flagged with references to conflicting KB entries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires multiple modules and real KB interaction, which is above Unit.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Whether the assistant hallucinates or adds a disclaimer is primarily model-behavior-dependent under missing-context conditions; mocks would not reveal this.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a thread requiring factual detail, force KB retrieval to return no relevant entries, then run real draft generation and assert presence of disclaimer language and absence of unsupported concrete claims.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-level deterministic test cannot validate hallucination-vs-disclaimer behavior.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure usually occurs in orchestration/state transitions between drafting, approval gate, and send adapter; pure unit tests can miss unintended send calls in wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute workflow with a non-approved draft and instrument the send adapter/outbox in an integrated test environment; assert zero send attempts before approval and exactly one after explicit approval.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers real module interactions around gating, which Unit would not reliably catch.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on multi-step agent behavior across sequential stages, including LLM output influencing later checks and approval presentation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a full agent session on a real email thread fixture through all stages (ingest, generate, fact-check, approval UI/state) and verify stage outputs/hand-offs and final blocked-until-approval behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This explicitly validates a multi-step AI agent run; lower levels would only test partial slices.
